task 1.content:
Task: Create Population Table in Google Sheets (GUI Interaction Only)

Your task is to use Google Sheets to create a structured table.
You must strictly use GUI interaction only (mouse clicks and keyboard input).
Do not use APIs, scripts, automation tools, browser console, extensions, or external tools.
Do not open or navigate to any unrelated website.

Table Structure

Create exactly four columns with the following column names (spelled exactly as written):

Year
Men (Millions)
Women (Millions)
Total (Millions)

Enter the Following Data

1995 – Men: 28.5, Women: 29.1, Total: 57.6
1996 – Men: 28.7, Women: 29.3, Total: 58.0
1997 – Men: 28.9, Women: 29.5, Total: 58.4
1998 – Men: 29.1, Women: 29.7, Total: 58.8
1999 – Men: 29.3, Women: 29.9, Total: 59.2
2000 – Men: 29.5, Women: 30.1, Total: 59.6
2001 – Men: 29.8, Women: 30.3, Total: 60.1
2002 – Men: 30.0, Women: 30.5, Total: 60.5
2003 – Men: 30.3, Women: 30.8, Total: 61.1
2004 – Men: 30.6, Women: 31.1, Total: 61.7
2005 – Men: 30.9, Women: 31.4, Total: 62.3
2006 – Men: 31.2, Women: 31.7, Total: 62.9
2007 – Men: 31.5, Women: 32.0, Total: 63.5
2008 – Men: 31.8, Women: 32.3, Total: 64.1
2009 – Men: 32.1, Women: 32.6, Total: 64.7
2010 – Men: 32.4, Women: 32.9, Total: 65.3
2011 – Men: 32.8, Women: 33.3, Total: 66.1
2012 – Men: 33.1, Women: 33.6, Total: 66.7
2013 – Men: 33.4, Women: 33.9, Total: 67.3
2014 – Men: 33.7, Women: 34.2, Total: 67.9
2015 – Men: 34.0, Women: 34.5, Total: 68.5
2016 – Men: 34.3, Women: 34.8, Total: 69.1
2017 – Men: 34.6, Women: 35.1, Total: 69.7
2018 – Men: 34.9, Women: 35.4, Total: 70.3
2019 – Men: 35.2, Women: 35.7, Total: 70.9
2020 – Men: 35.5, Women: 36.0, Total: 71.5
2021 – Men: 35.8, Women: 36.3, Total: 72.1
2022 – Men: 36.1, Women: 36.6, Total: 72.7
2023 – Men: 36.4, Women: 36.9, Total: 73.3
2024 – Men: 36.7, Women: 37.2, Total: 73.9

Requirements

Column names must match exactly.

All 30 rows from 1995 to 2024 must be present.

Totals must be entered correctly.

Do not modify column names.

Do not skip any year.

Use only visible GUI actions inside Google Sheets.

task 2.content: Your task is to use Gmail and Google Calendar to perform a sequence of actions strictly within a single browser tab. You must not open any new tabs or windows at any point during the task. First, open Gmail and from there navigate to Google Calendar without creating a new tab. Block your calendar for the entire day on 31 March 2026 by creating an all-day event and saving it. After successfully saving the event, return to Gmail in the same tab and compose an email to yourself with the subject line "Calendar Blocked – 31 March 2026" and a message stating that the calendar has been successfully blocked for the full day on 31 March 2026. Send the email. Then, still using the same browser tab, navigate back to Google Calendar, locate the all-day event created for 31 March 2026, and delete it. Confirm that the event has been completely removed before ending the task. Under no circumstances should you open a new tab, use background tabs, or switch to a separate window.

task 3.content: Use Microsoft Edge to open the Asda.co.uk website. Click on the sign-in button, sign in. The email and password will be autofilled — click sign in. After signing in, search for 'tomato' and add the cheapest regular, no can tomato to the basket. Then search for and add avocado, then bread, then egg. After adding all items, check the total price. Then navigate to an online notepad and enter the exact name of the tomato that was selected, the exact name of the avocado that was selected, the exact name of the bread that was selected, the exact name of the egg that was selected, and the price of each item.

task 4.content: Use Microsoft Edge. Open Skyscanner.co.uk and search for a one-way flight from Mumbai to New York on 15 April 2026 for 1 passenger — select the cheapest direct flight. Then search for a one-way flight from New York to Tokyo on 20 April 2026 for 1 passenger — select the cheapest direct flight. Then search for a return flight from Manchester to Dubai, departing 1 May 2026 and returning 8 May 2026, for 1 passenger. After completing all three searches, open an online notepad and write a short poem about flying. Then go back to Skyscanner and retrieve the full details of the first flight searched — including airline name, departure time, arrival time, duration, and price. Return to the online notepad and enter the complete details of that flight.

task 5.content:
Use Microsoft Edge. Open https://onlinenotepad.org/notepad and type the following poem exactly as written, including all spelling errors:

Mornning Light

When mornning light beggins to rise,
It paints soft colors in the skyes.
The air is cool, the world half-wake,
As golden streems the darknes break.

A quitet hush on fields of grean,
With dewdrops shinning, fresh and clean.
The sparows call from branch to tree,
Their songs drift slow and wild and free.

For even when the skys seem gray,
The light will find its brite new way.

After typing the entire poem, go back to the very beginning and correct the spelling of each misspelled word one by one, working through the entire poem from start to finish.

task 6.content: Use Microsoft Edge. Navigate to https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/amar5693/student-performance-dataset/code and download the dataset as a zip file. Extract the zip file and then open Google Colab. Upload the extracted dataset directly into Colab — no terminal usage is allowed. Explore the dataset at a high level to understand its structure and key features. After understanding the dataset, write a simple machine learning model using Random Forest to predict the marks of the student.
